title: camellia assamica subsp. lasiocalyx
intro: Camellia assamica subsp. lasiocalyx, also known as the Yunnan Gold tea plant, is a subspecies of Camellia assamica, a type of evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of Yunnan Province in China. The leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Camellia assamica subsp. lasiocalyx include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into tea, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal properties and health benefits.
